question: Geological classification On the process of formation, rocks are classified as igneous rocks, sedimentary rocks and metamorphic rocks.

options.a: Igneous Rocks: These are the rocks formed by cooling of magma. These rocks are strong and
options.b: Sedimentary Rocks: Disintegrated rock material is carried by flowing water and deposited
options.c: Metamorphic Rocks: These are the rocks formed after igneous rocks and sedimentary rocks

subject: General Civil Engineering
question: Physical classification Based on the structure, rocks may be classified as stratified, unstratified and foliated rocks.

options.a: Stratified Rocks: They have layered structure. Examples: Sandstones, limestones, mud-stones.
options.b: Unstratified Rocks: They possess crystalline and compact grains. Examples: Granite, trap,
options.c: Foliated Rocks: They have foliated structure. Along the planes of foliation they split easily.

subject: General Civil Engineering
question: Chemical classification

options.a: Siliceous Rocks: Main constituent is silica. These rocks are hard and durable. Examples: Granite,
options.b: Argillaceous Rocks: Clay is the main constituent. These rocks are brittle. They cannot withstand
options.c: Calcareous Rocks: Calcium carbonate is the main constituent. Examples: Limestone, marble.

question: Classification based on durability: Durability test is conducted by Forest Research Institute of India, Dehradun by burrying 600 × 50 × 50 size specimen upto half their length and observing them over several years. Then timber is classified as

options.a: High durability: Average life of more than 10 years
options.b: Moderate durability: Average life 5 to 10 years
options.c: Low durability: Average life is less than 5 years.
